# PillAp { #astroapers.PillAp }

```python
PillAp(positions, w, a, b, theta=0.0, plot_samples=96, *, validate=True)
```

Capsule-like aperture composed from one rectangle and two ellipses.

The pill aperture has a central rectangle of length ``w`` and height
``2 * b`` with elliptical caps of semiaxes ``a`` and ``b`` centered on the
rectangle ends. Its masks use fused Rust kernels that preserve the package's
composite rectangle-plus-cap semantics.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**positions**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple or array - like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Aperture center or centers as ``(x, y)`` pixel coordinates. Multiple centers are accepted as an ``(N, 2)`` array-like object.

<code>[**w**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Length of the central rectangle in pixels, measured along the pill major axis between the two cap centers.

<code>[**a**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Semimajor axis of each elliptical cap in pixels.

<code>[**b**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Semiminor axis of each elliptical cap in pixels. The central rectangle height is ``2 * b``.

<code>[**theta**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Rotation angle in radians, measured counterclockwise from the positive x axis.

<code>[**plot_samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [96]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Even number of samples used to draw the two elliptical caps when creating Matplotlib patches.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `False`, skip input validation and assume finite, positive geometry and normalized positions.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**area**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Analytic geometric area, ``w * 2 * b + pi * a * b``. Image clipping and bad-pixel masks are accounted for by ``npix`` results, not by this property.

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [apsum](#astroapers.PillAp.apsum) | Return pill-aperture sums, and npix by default. |

### apsum { #astroapers.PillAp.apsum }

```python
PillAp.apsum(data, mask=None, *, method='exact', return_npix=True)
```

Return pill-aperture sums, and npix by default.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**data**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Two-dimensional image.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with the same shape as ``data``. ``True`` pixels are excluded from both ``apsum`` and ``npix``.

<code>[**return_npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `True`, return ``(apsum, npix)``. If `False`, return only ``apsum``.
